solution: Write the right side as a power of [[b]]: \( [[rhs]] = [[b]]^{[[E]]} \). Since the bases match, \( [[a]]x + ([[c]]) = [[E]] \), so \( x = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This solves \( [[a]]x + c = [[E]] \) but forgets to divide by the coefficient [[a]].

solution: Rewrite both sides with the common base [[c]]: \( [[base1]] = [[c]]^{[[m]]} \) and \( [[base2]] = [[c]]^{[[n]]} \), so the equation becomes \( [[c]]^{[[m]]x} = [[c]]^{[[n]][[k]]} \). Matching exponents, \( [[m]]x = [[n]]([[k]]) \), so \( x = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reports the given exponent \( k \) directly, without converting to the common base.

solution: Matching bases means the exponents must be equal: \( x + ([[c1]]) = [[a]]x + ([[c2]]) \). Collecting the \( x \) terms gives \( x(1 - [[a]]) = [[c2]] - [[c1]] \), so \( x = \dfrac{[[c2]] - [[c1]]}{1 - [[a]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reports the difference of the constants directly, forgetting the coefficient of \( x \) is not 1 on both sides.
This has the numerator's subtraction reversed relative to the denominator's sign convention.
This has the correct value but the wrong sign.

stem: Solve \( [[b]]^{2x} - ([[s]])\cdot [[b]]^{x} + [[prod]] = 0 \). Which of the following is a solution for \( x \)?
solution: Substituting \( y = [[b]]^{x} \) gives the quadratic \( y^{2} - ([[s]])y + [[prod]] = 0 \), which factors as \( (y - [[b]]^{[[p]]})(y - [[b]]^{[[q]]}) = 0 \). So \( [[b]]^{x} = [[b]]^{[[p]]} \) or \( [[b]]^{x} = [[b]]^{[[q]]} \), giving \( x = [[p]] \) or \( x = [[q]] \); one solution is \( x = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This reports the sum \( s = b^{p}+b^{q}\) itself rather than either exponent solution.
This adds the two exponent solutions instead of reporting a single one of them.
This multiplies the two exponent solutions instead of reporting a single one of them.
This is well outside the two genuine solutions.
This is well outside the two genuine solutions.
This is well outside the two genuine solutions.

stem: What is the smallest integer \( x \) for which \( [[b]]^{x} > [[N]] \)?
